mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly supporting higher equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained monthly decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals accelerating labor market tightening, which boosts consumer spending and corporate profit expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) when unemployment falls below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over individual series in a multivariate regression (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding its 12-month moving average by more than 3% signals supply-side capacity constraints and impending inflationary pressures, prompting risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of triggered instances, or the signal reverses direction during periods of accommodative monetary policy.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.3% triggers expectations of central bank policy tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance (t-stat < 1.96) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls provides a direct measure of economic momentum, generating positive short-term market sentiment and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of observations, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% confirms robust aggregate demand, which supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive returns in >60% of triggered quarters, or predictive accuracy drops to baseline levels when GDP growth exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and contraction in the unemployment rate indicates synchronized supply and labor market strength, which reinforces earnings expectations and drives positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate model, or forward returns are negative in >55% of joint-signal instances.
